summary:
Physicians often fail to fully meet the needs of family caregivers due to systemic challenges like limited time and poor training, though they increasingly recognize caregiver burdens through formal screenings and integrated interventions such as respite referrals and educational programs. These efforts indicate growing structure and intentionality in addressing caregiver needs within routine geriatric care—supporting an intermediate assessment rather than outright failure or success.  

$\boxed{C}$
